questions.question: In 2D DP, if dp[i][j] depends only on dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], and dp[i-1][j-1], which space optimization is possible?
options: Reduce from O(mn) to O(m + n) using two rows
No optimization is possible
Reduce from O(mn) to O(min(m,n)) using a single rolling row and one variable
Reduce from O(mn) to O(1) using mathematical formulas
questions.explanation: Since dp[i][j] only needs the previous row (i-1), we can keep just one row of length n and update it in-place, reducing space to O(n) or O(min(m,n)) after swapping to keep the shorter dimension as the inner one.

questions.question: When performing traceback in the LCS algorithm, if a[i-1] != b[j-1] and dp[i-1][j] >= dp[i][j-1], which direction do you move?
options: Move diagonally to dp[i-1][j-1]
Move up to dp[i-1][j] (skip character in a)
Move left to dp[i][j-1] (skip character in b)
Either direction is valid — the LCS is not unique
questions.explanation: When characters don't match, you follow the direction of the larger subproblem. Since dp[i-1][j] >= dp[i][j-1], the optimal LCS is in the upper cell, meaning you skip a[i-1] by moving up.

questions.question: The 0/1 knapsack recurrence is dp[i][w] = max(dp[i-1][w], dp[i-1][w-wt[i]] + val[i]). What changes in the unbounded knapsack?
options: The second term becomes dp[i][w-wt[i]] + val[i] (same row, not previous row)
The first term becomes dp[i+1][w] (next row)
Nothing changes — unbounded is identical to 0/1
Both terms use dp[i][w] on the right side
questions.explanation: In unbounded knapsack, items can be reused, so including item i means you stay on the same row i (can use it again). In 0/1, you move to row i-1 to prevent reuse.

questions.question: Matrix chain multiplication has state dp[i][j] = min cost to multiply A_i through A_j. What is the time complexity and why?
options: O(n²) because there are n² subproblems
O(n³) because each of the O(n²) subproblems tries O(n) split points
O(n⁴) because each split requires merging two O(n²) tables
O(2^n) because each matrix can be grouped in exponentially many ways
questions.explanation: There are O(n²) pairs (i,j) and for each you try up to O(n) split points k, giving O(n³) total. The O(n²) subproblem count alone ignores the O(n) work per subproblem.

questions.question: In the classic longest common subsequence (LCS) problem, what does dp[i][j] typically represent?
options: The length of the longest common prefix of the first i and j characters
The length of the LCS of the first i characters of one string and first j of the other
The edit distance between the two strings
The number of matching characters at positions i and j
questions.explanation: In the standard LCS DP formulation, dp[i][j] stores the length of the longest common subsequence between the first i characters of string X and the first j characters of string Y. This builds up from dp[0][0] = 0 using the recurrence based on whether characters match.
